bead.title: prompts: extract shared instruction blocks (byte-equivalent refactor)
bead.description:
PROBLEM
executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText (1078 words) and executeBeadInstructionsAgentText (1020 words) duplicate ~250 words across Step 0, evidence steering, review-gate, bead-override, and constraints tail. Maintaining two parallel prompt texts means changes to shared logic require dual edits and risk divergence.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (or prompts package): two large string constants define the Claude and Agent harness instructions. Approximately 250 words appear verbatim in both, including the Step 0 investigation guidance, evidence steering instructions, review-gate checklist, bead-override pattern, and constraints tail.
- No shared constant or template mechanism exists; both strings are inline.
- ddx-cfedee8e (Story 10 prompt edits, a dep) must land first to avoid merge conflict.

PROPOSED FIX
- Extract the shared ~250 words into named constants (e.g., sharedStep0Block, sharedEvidenceBlock, sharedReviewGateBlock, sharedBeadOverrideBlock, sharedConstraintsTailBlock).
- Reassemble both Claude and Agent variants as: per-harness preamble + sharedStep0Block + process body (harness-specific) + shared tail blocks.
- Byte-equivalent: output bytes of the assembled prompts must match the prior output exactly (golden snapshot test).

NON-SCOPE
- Semantic changes to any prompt instruction.
- Changing prompt selection logic or harness routing.
bead.acceptance:
1. Shared block constants extracted (at minimum: step0, evidence steering, review-gate, bead-override, constraints tail).
2. executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText and executeBeadInstructionsAgentText reassembled from blocks + per-harness preamble.
3. TestPrompts_ClaudeVariant_ByteIdentical: golden snapshot test verifies Claude output bytes match prior version.
4. TestPrompts_AgentVariant_ByteIdentical: golden snapshot test verifies Agent output bytes match prior version.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

events.body: {"rationale":"Strong bead overall. Title is imperative and names subsystem + change. Description has PROBLEM, ROOT CAUSE with file:line reference, PROPOSED FIX, and NON-SCOPE. AC has numbered items with specific test function names (TestPrompts_ClaudeVariant_ByteIdentical, TestPrompts_AgentVariant_ByteIdentical), go test gate, and lefthook gate. Labels include phase, story, area, kind. Parent and dep are explicit. Minor gaps: (1) the file:line reference is approximate ('cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (or prompts package)' — 'or' suggests the author hasn't confirmed the exact file); (2) AC item 5 uses './internal/agent/...' but the actual package location should be verified against the codebase; (3) no explicit 'no deps beyond ddx-cfedee8e' statement — the dep is listed but the description only mentions it as context, not as a hard prerequisite in the AC or deps field; (4) the golden snapshot test approach is sound but AC does not specify where the snapshots live or how to regenerate them, which could block a sub-agent.","score":7,"suggested_fixes":["Confirm the exact file path for the two string constants (grep for executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText) and replace 'cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go (or prompts package)' with the confirmed path and line number.","Add an AC line specifying snapshot file location and regeneration command (e.g., 'Snapshots stored under cli/internal/agent/testdata/; regenerate with go test -update').","Tighten dep note: add a line in description 'Deps: ddx-cfedee8e must be merged before this bead is dispatched to avoid merge conflict.'","Verify the go test path './internal/agent/...' matches the actual package layout (run 'cd cli \u0026\u0026 go list ./internal/agent/...' to confirm)."],"waivers_applied":[]}
